text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Beth. It smells delicious, sizzling in oil and it's great for keeping vampires away.
zh: 我是尼尔。我是贝丝。它在油里滋滋作响,闻起来香气四溢,还能有效驱赶吸血鬼。

text: But don't eat it on a first date. I'm talking, of course, about garlic.
zh: 但可别在初次约会时吃它。我说的当然就是大蒜。

text: Neil, do you like garlic? Oh yes, I love garlic.
zh: 尼尔,你喜欢大蒜吗?哦,当然,我超爱大蒜。

text: I had a very garlicky meal last night. Mmm, I know I can smell it from here!
zh: 我昨晚吃了一顿蒜味十足的饭。嗯,我在这儿都能闻到味儿了!

text: is Jason Chan, an Australian chef who specialises in Asian cuisine, and Paul -Erik Jensen,
zh: 是专门研究亚洲菜的澳大利亚厨师Jason Chan,以及居住在法国的丹麦厨师Paul-Erik Jensen,

text: a Danish chef living in France, talking about their love of garlic to BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain.
zh: 他们在BBC世界服务频道的节目《The Food Chain》中,谈到了他们对大蒜的喜爱。

text: I love garlic because it's a versatile ingredient that can be used for cooking and offers various health benefits.
zh: 我喜欢大蒜,因为它是一种用途广泛的食材,既能用于烹饪,又有多种健康益处。

text: We use it a lot. So, from stocks to soups and in vegetable dishes, meat dishes, there's very certain a clove of garlic in
zh: 我们经常用它。从高汤到汤品,再到蔬菜料理、肉类料理,几乎肯定都能找到一瓣大蒜的踪影。

text: somewhere. Yeah, it's unimaginable not using garlic.
zh: 是啊,简直无法想象做饭不用大蒜。

text: Jason loves garlic because it's versatile, meaning it can be used in many different ways.
zh: Jason喜欢大蒜,因为它用途多样,也就是说它可以用在很多不同的地方。

text: And Paul thinks cooking without garlic is unimaginable, meaning it's difficult to imagine because it would be so bad.
zh: 而Paul认为,做饭不用大蒜简直难以想象,意思是这种情形太糟糕了,让人难以设想。

text: love garlic or hate it. In this episode, we'll be finding out more about this
zh: 有人爱大蒜,也有人讨厌它。在本期节目中,我们将进一步了解这种

text: versatile, strong -smelling food. And as usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases as well.
zh: 用途广泛、气味浓烈的食物。像往常一样,我们也会学习一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: And remember, you can find all the vocabulary from this episode on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 别忘了,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本期节目的所有词汇。

text: Now, Beth, I have a question for you. It's a little -known fact that of the 600 varieties of garlic,
zh: 好了,贝丝,我有个问题要问你。有个鲜为人知的事实:在600个大蒜品种中,

text: many grew only in the former Soviet Union and were were unavailable in the West until the fall of communism in the 1990s.
zh: 有许多只生长在前苏联地区,直到20世纪90年代共产主义体制瓦解之前,西方国家都无法获得它们。

text: So, which of the following varieties of garlic grew in the former USSR?
zh: 那么,以下哪个大蒜品种是在前苏联生长的呢?

text: Is it… a rock 'n 'ball b fire or c riesig?
zh: 是……a. rock'n'ball,b. fire,还是c. riesig?

text: Oh, I have no idea. I'm going to guess fire.
zh: 哦,我完全不知道。我猜是fire。

text: OK, we'll find out if you're right at the end of the programme. Garlic is much more than just a cooking ingredient.
zh: 好,我们会在节目结尾揭晓答案是否正确。大蒜远不止是一种烹饪食材。

text: For centuries, garlic was an important food throughout ancient Egypt, Greece and Rome, as well as India and China.
zh: 几个世纪以来,大蒜在古埃及、古希腊、古罗马,以及印度和中国都是重要的食物。

text: It was the Roman Empire that brought garlic to European countries like France.
zh: 正是罗马帝国把大蒜带到了法国等欧洲国家。

text: And everywhere garlic grew, it was praised for its health -giving powers.
zh: 而无论大蒜生长在哪里,人们都称赞它有益健康的功效。

text: Here, BBC presenter Rumela Dasgupta asks author and garlic expert Robin Cherry
zh: 在这里,BBC主持人Rumela Dasgupta向作家兼大蒜专家Robin Cherry

text: about garlic's medicinal uses on BBC World Service's The Food Chain.
zh: 请教了大蒜的药用价值,访谈内容来自BBC世界服务频道节目《The Food Chain》。

text: And what kind of illnesses or conditions would they have treated, for instance?
zh: 举例来说,大蒜曾被用来治疗哪些疾病或病症呢?

text: Everything from cancer, scurvy, the plague, respiratory ailments.
zh: 从癌症、坏血病、瘟疫,到呼吸系统疾病,几乎无所不包。

text: It was used as an aphrodisiac. Garlic was used to treat respiratory ailments.
zh: 它还被当作一种春药使用。大蒜被用来治疗呼吸系统疾病。

text: An ailment is another word for an illness or minor health problem. So a respiratory ailment is an illness of the lungs.
zh: Ailment(病症)是illness(疾病)或轻微健康问题的另一种说法。所以respiratory ailment指的是肺部疾病。

text: In ancient Greece, garlic was also used as an aphrodisiac, a food or drink believed to increase sexual desire.
zh: 在古希腊,大蒜也被当作春药使用,也就是一种被认为能增强性欲的食物或饮品。

text: But interestingly, this was the same reason why some cultures avoided eating garlic
zh: 但有趣的是,正是出于同样的原因,一些文化群体完全避免食用大蒜,

text: altogether, including a religious group from India called Jains.
zh: 其中包括印度一个叫耆那教徒的宗教群体。

text: Yes, Jainism is an ancient Indian religion,
zh: 是的,耆那教是一种古老的印度宗教,

text: like Hinduism and Buddhism, based on the idea of uhimsa, or non -violence.
zh: 与印度教和佛教一样,建立在“阿希姆萨”(即非暴力)理念之上。

text: But unlike many Hindus and Buddhists, Jains' definition of non -violence includes plants and vegetables.
zh: 但与许多印度教徒和佛教徒不同的是,耆那教徒对非暴力的理解还包括植物和蔬菜。

text: Jains don't eat garlic because uprooting the plant kills both it and the soil ecosystem it grows in.
zh: 耆那教徒不吃大蒜,因为拔出这种植物会同时破坏它本身以及它所生长的土壤生态系统。

text: Here's Sanal Ved, a Mumbai -based cooking show host and author of many books on Indian cuisine,
zh: 这位是Sanal Ved,一位常驻孟买的烹饪节目主持人,也是多部印度美食著作的作者,

text: explaining another reason why Jains avoid garlic to BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain.
zh: 他在BBC世界服务频道节目《The Food Chain》中,解释了耆那教徒避免食用大蒜的另一个原因。

text: According to Hinduism also, garlic is considered a food which is called the Tamsik food.
zh: 根据印度教的说法,大蒜也被视为一种“Tamsik”食物。

text: It is believed to stimulate desire, passion, aggression.
zh: 人们相信它会刺激欲望、激情和攻击性。

text: And that's why they abstain from eating those kinds of food,
zh: 这也是为什么他们会戒食这类食物,

text: because it is believed to impact your spiritual growth and also your mental clarity.
zh: 因为人们相信它会影响人的精神修养和心智清明。

text: Jains abstain from eating garlic. If you abstain from doing something, you choose not to do it.
zh: 耆那教徒戒食大蒜。如果你abstain from(戒除)做某事,就是选择不去做它。

text: For example, you might abstain from tobacco or alcohol because you know they're bad for your health.
zh: 比如,你可能会戒烟或戒酒,因为你知道它们对健康有害。

text: Like the ancient Greeks, Janes believed that garlic stimulates sexual desire.
zh: 和古希腊人一样,耆那教徒也相信大蒜会刺激性欲。

text: The verb to stimulate means encourage it or cause it to develop and grow.
zh: 动词stimulate(刺激)的意思是促使某事发展或增长。

text: Modern medicine has confirmed some of these ancient beliefs
zh: 现代医学已经证实了一些关于大蒜健康益处的古老说法,

text: about the health benefits of garlic, including reduced blood pressure and better gut health.
zh: 其中包括降低血压和改善肠道健康。

text: OK, Neil, isn't it time you revealed the answer to your question?
zh: 好了,尼尔,是不是该揭晓你那个问题的答案了?

text: Yes, Beth, I asked you which variety of garlic grew in the former USSR. And I said fire.
zh: 是的,贝丝,我问你前苏联生长的是哪种大蒜,你答的是fire。

text: Was I right? You were absolutely correct.
zh: 我说得对吗?你说得完全正确。

text: Fire or Georgian fire is a hot white garlic from the Republic of Georgia in the former Soviet Union.
zh: Fire,也就是“Georgian fire”(格鲁吉亚火蒜),是产自前苏联格鲁吉亚共和国的一种辛辣白蒜。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with versatile – an adjective meaning to have many different purposes or
zh: 好,我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,首先是versatile——一个形容词,意思是具有多种用途或

text: uses. The phrase it's unimaginable is used to say that something is difficult to imagine because it would be so good or so bad.
zh: 功能。短语it's unimaginable用来形容某件事好到或坏到让人难以想象。

text: An ailment is an illness or health problem. An aphrodisiac is a food or drink believed to increase sexual desire.
zh: Ailment指疾病或健康问题。Aphrodisiac指一种被认为能增强性欲的食物或饮品。

text: If you abstain from something, you choose not to do it.
zh: 如果你abstain from某事,就是选择不去做它。

text: And finally, to stimulate means to encourage something or cause it to grow.
zh: 最后,stimulate的意思是促使某事发展或增长。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up. If you've enjoyed this episode, then head over to our website bbclearningenglish .com,
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了。如果你喜欢这期节目,欢迎访问我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,

text: where you'll find a quiz and worksheet to practice the vocabulary you've learnt.
zh: 那里有测验和练习题,帮助你巩固所学的词汇。

text: See you again soon, but for now it's Goodbye.
zh: 我们下次再见,现在先说再见啦。
